The Ḥanbalī jurist Ibn Taymiyya (Taqī al-Dīn Aḥmad ibn Taymiyya, 661–728 AH / 1263–1328 CE) authored the famous Minhāj al-Sunna al-Nabawiyya. His student Ibn Qayyim al-Jawziyya (Shams al-Dīn Muḥammad ibn Abī Bakr) developed his teacher's ideas on maṣlaḥa (public interest) and the objectives of Islamic law (maqāṣid al-sharīʿa). Both scholars emphasized returning to primary Scriptural sources over later scholastic elaboration (taqlīd).
